request.problem_description: Given heights, return the maximum area formed by two lines.

ideal_response:
## Diagnosis
The pointer movement rule is reversed.

## Likely Bug Location
The branch that moves `left` when `height[left] > height[right]`.

## Why It Fails
The limiting side determines the current area. Moving the taller side keeps the smaller limiting height and can skip better candidates.

## Failing Test Walkthrough
On the sample, moving the taller side too early prevents the search from preserving the left height of 8 long enough to pair with the later 7.

## Suggested Fix
Move the pointer on the shorter side after evaluating the area.

## Complexity
The intended two-pointer solution is O(n) time and O(1) space.

request.problem_description: Given piles and h hours, return the minimum integer eating speed to finish all bananas within h hours.
request.constraints: piles length and pile values are positive. h is at least piles.length.

ideal_response:
## Hint
Think about a way to test whether a proposed speed is fast enough.

## Why This Helps
Once you can check one speed, you can search over the range of possible speeds instead of trying every schedule.

## Next Step
Write down the smallest and largest possible eating speeds before choosing the search strategy.
